page: Microsoft Azure EC4eds_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the ECEDV5 server family. This instance runs on the x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel processor and is managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs and 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core.

The server includes 108 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage options for external disk attachments. It does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses. There are no onboard graphics processing units on this instance, making it reliant entirely on its dedicated Intel CPU cores for all computational tasks.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the EC4eds_v5 offers a dense memory allocation relative to its processing core count, which is typical for memory-optimized hardware classes. This resource density allows for efficient handling of memory-bound workloads without paying for unnecessary CPU cores. However, the lack of integrated GPU acceleration and the moderate 108 GB local storage capacity represent clear tradeoffs for workloads requiring massive local data caching or parallelized graphics processing.

This virtual machine is designed for workloads that require substantial memory capacity relative to compute power. Appropriate use cases include relational database servers, in-memory caching layers, medium-scale enterprise applications, and data analytics pipelines that fit within the 32.0 GB memory envelope.
description: Microsoft Azure EC4eds_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine from the ECEDV5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is equipped with 4 dedicated Intel vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM, establishing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. For storage, the instance features 108 GB of local disk and supports Premium Storage configurations. It does not include integrated GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Qualitatively, the server provides high memory density relative to its processing cores, offering a cost-efficient profile for memory-bound tasks without the overhead of excess CPU capacity. Tradeoffs include the absence of hardware acceleration and moderate local storage. This configuration is suitable for relational databases, in-memory caches, and data processing workloads.
